This is unfortunate. I wonder what will happen next? "If someone means us harm, we will crush them."
It's strange... I spoke to Huan Ch'ang before we set off, and even she couldn't foretell the coming of any storm.
The storm has made it hard to reach the Commander, too... We'll just have to wait here until we get orders.
Yes, I wonder what the Commander is doing... Until we're told otherwise, we'll just have to do what we can.
Cruise Ship - Bridge
Commander, a storm of indeterminate origin has appeared. I am analyzing it but as of yet unable to explain it.
A hologram of TB appears above the table.
A simple system backup of TB was performed on the ship so TB, too, could enjoy the cruise.
Normally, she wouldn't have to take any action, since all the work on the ship is done by shipgirls coordinating with each other.
This, needless to say, is not a normal circumstance.
TB, were there any errors in our observation records?
Processing order... There were no errors. This storm appeared spontaneously.
Based on latest observations, there is a high probability that a large octopod known as a "kraken" is present in the storm.
Really? A high probability?
Yes. By comparing its appearance with that of similar entities in my database, the most likely match was determined to be this legendary creature.
It is worth noting that the thermal imager and radar are not malfunctioning. A new course has been calculated and may be initiated at any time.
Additionally, defensive measures have been planned out, incorporating the firepower of the shipgirls aboard.
If necessary, the vessel is prepared to enter combat.
Just keep your eyes on the situation for now. There's something I want to check.
Orders received.
Meanwhile, on the deck...
Geryon says tentacles are emerging from the storm. Strangely, though, the tentacles show no sign of aggression.
They aren't hostile to us?
CREEEAK!
The ship rocked fiercely. A rally of tentacles extended from the storm, gently wrapping themselves around the ship.
I... think these tentacles may belong to someone from Tempesta.
I refuse to believe a sea creature of this size is anyone's pet. Furthermore, they are clearly targeting the ship.
If it IS Tempesta, it's all the more suspicious! They're clearly after something, seeing as they aren't attacking!
We're talking about pirates. They could be after one thing.
No, you don't mean...?
The Commander!
(I knew it...)
As the tentacles shake the ship's hull, they creep along the railings and slither their way from the bridge onto the deck.
Brace for the storm, everyone!
The winds grow in fury as the rain pours down. As thunderbolts strike without pause, a few shadowy figures appear deep within the storm.
The dire situation gets even worse when we realize that a fleet has surrounded the ship, cutting off all escape routes.
Heheh. By sailing in these seas, you've already signed a pact to surrender everything you have!
Have you heard the tale? "When a sudden storm brews up and black sails flutter in the wind, that is when the great captains shall return."
For the uninitiated–
That means us evil, evil pirates have you completely surrounded♡
Oi! You stole my line, Hind!
Forgive me, dear. I just couldn't resist the urge to finish your sentence. Go on, say the rest, Fortune.
Ahem... Listen up, people! We can do this the easy way, or the hard way! Either way, you and your ship are coming with us!
(Seems I was wrong. We did run into a tempest after all.)
(I'm surprised Tempesta are actually practicing piracy. Not that it's off-brand for them... I suppose I'll play along for now.)
Goddamn pirates! Of all the hands we'd fall into, I can't believe it's yours!
You're going to abduct us, huh? Then what are you going to do to us?
That's obvious. You're all gonna walk the pla– I mean, we're gonna ransom you off!
And who's going to pay you the ransom if you've already made a hostage of everyone who could do so?
You... can't pay the ransom? Fine, then we'll liquidate that posh ship you've– No, that won't work either! You'll just have to pay with your BODY, Commander!
Well, well, you've let the cat out of the bag now, Fortune!
Aw, shoot... Hey, wipe that grin off your face! I couldn't help myself, alright?
*yaaawn*... I'm shleepy. Can we get this over with?
How did you find us?
Uhh, we...
We really, really missed you, my darling! I looked all over for you, and now I've finally found you.
And my, looks like we caught you on a vacation. If you don't have anywhere else to be, why don't you come visit us?
I DO like the sound of that... but first, I should hear what everyone else thinks.
The answer is unanimous – they all want to visit Tempesta's secret retreat. And so, it becomes our next destination.
The giant tentacles let go of the vessel, and the pirates graciously adjust our course.
Our ship and theirs cruise onward, to the center of the storm that still bursts with flashes and lightning.
Grab something and hold on tight, now!
Ahoy! The door to the New World will open any second now!
Our ship thrusts straight into the storm's center as Royal Fortune boisterously laughs.

To Tempesta's Retreat.say: Soon after we weather the storm, an unfamiliar port comes into view.
Is this... Tempesta's new base of operations?
Why, naturally. It'd be difficult to conduct business around these parts if we didn't have some sort of springboard, so we decided to relocate after a little cost-benefit analysis~
As for what kind of business... You should already be quite familiar with that, so let's not worry about this matter any longer~
(I guess that stuff comes part and parcel with being a pirate. It might be better not to pry after all...)
(Now that I think about it, though, we've welcomed quite a few guests from other worlds before. Funny that the shoe's on the other foot this time.)
After dropping anchors and mooring the ship, our crew officially sets foot onto this unknown land.
The Tempesta members barely have a chance to introduce the situation with the new secret retreat before a lively conversation breaks out.
Oh yeah, what were you guys planning to do anyway if you hadn't run into us?
Oh, umm... I think we were planning t-to take a trip around the tropics...
But, things are way more fun when they don't go as planned, right? Heehee~
A trip around the tropics, huh? Sounds pretty nice to me~ Might even be a good opportunity to find something special for my collection.
Having that said, as nice as the tropical scenery might be, there's nothing unique or special about it.
In other words, you're chiefly interested in seeking out something unforgettable?
Yeah, more or less~
...I'm not sure that's something you can count on.
I guess you've got a point. Well, we can always save this discussion for later.
Mmh. If it's unforgettable sights you seek, you'll not want to miss out on the auroras.
Auroras?
Yes, magnificent bands of light that hang down like curtains from the skies.
Such dazzling, vivid colors... These phenomena can be observed from both poles, and it's said that a single glance at their spectacular nightglow will burn the scenery into your memory forever.
Of course, it'll be very cold, but you'll forget all about that the moment you see the aurora.
Woah, I'm getting fired up just from hearing about it! That sounds like one of those adventures worth putting your life on the line for!
I'm sure a photo of the aurora would make an excellent addition to my collection~♪
I'll handle the photography! I'll make sure to capture every breathtaking moment!
Hrm? Are you sure your phone will work in the sheer cold? The electronics might freeze over or something.
*gasp*... You're right! I'll ask Ingraham if she can do something about it...
Well, since we've all been talking about the same thing for a while now, why don't we formally set course for the polar south?
The Tempesta crew seems to be on board as well, and opportunities to hang out and try something new don't come around too often. Let's all head down there together, yeah?
Alrighty then! Let's vote on it, pirate-style! If everyone agrees on the same thing, we'll ask Commander to change course!
Now then, can I see a show of hands? All in favour–
Everyone present raises their hands. Royal Fortune and Golden Hind turn to me triumphantly.
(Okay, there are no logistical issues. We're fully resupplied from Seabreeze Island, so... we've got ourselves our next destination.)
Booyah! You're the best, Commander!
H-hey, easy there, Royal Fortune! No need to glomp me!
Just as the merry discussion is starting to wrap up, I suddenly hear someone's stomach growling.
So hungry... Time to tap into my emergency supply of crisps and cola... Be right back, running to my room real quick...
I don't think that potato chips and cola counts as a proper meal... Why not head back to the cruise ship? There's plenty of food on board.
It'd be a pity to turn back around as soon as we got here. How about we whip up something to eat here?
Hmm... That sounds pretty promising. How does a bonfire barbecue sound?
You know, a setting where everyone can sit around the bonfire, chat freely, and get to know each other while eating. It sure beats a stuffy dinner banquet – right, Commander?
I'm fine with that, but isn't it a bit early for that?
What? Did anyone ever make a rule that you can't have a bonfire barbecue in broad daylight? Besides, it's the will of the people~
A barbecue sounds nice... but I could do without the bonfire...
Sorry, but that's not an option~ You'll be joining us as well, Mary, and I can't have you disappearing on us~
Eep! Commander, save meeeee!
Despite it being way too early in the day, our "Big Bonfire Barbecue Bonanza" kicked off without a hitch.
Under the direction of Royal Fortune, Lung Wu and the rest of the cooking crew begin bringing out the meat.
Littorio, Sovetsky Soyuz, and Adventure Galley supply the drinks.
The high-quality ham that São Martinho brought over is sliced thinly, then paired together with Owari's freshly-farmed melons for a light and savory snack.
Laughter fills the air around this strange port, and everyone quickly comes to deepen their understanding of each other.
Before I realize it, the barbecue bonanza is already winding down, and someone signals its end by sticking a sword into the bonfire.
I am most satisfied with the success of this bonfire barbecue, but there's still something lacking to truly make this day spectacular.
That's right! To truly elevate today's atmosphere... we require a ballroom dancing corner!
Yes, yes. Let's make our preparations in the main hall! Bel, gather the Royal Maids and have them decorate the main hall accordingly!
As you wish, Your Majesty.
Wasn't into ballroom dancin' back then. Still ain't now.
Excuse me? Does this Iris swashbuckler have any better ideas? If not, do as I say!
Ballroom dancing is THE symbol of elegance and high class. Us piratey types don't mesh well with that, you know.
If you really want to dance that badly, why not opt for something like a masquerade ball?
Yeah, that could work~ If anything, a masquerade ball would allow us to accentuate our pirate-ness~
Uhhh... both options are equally a pain in the bum.
I'm also in favour of a masquerade ball.
Woah, that's a basically a costume party, right? I need to go grab my explorer's outfit and robo hunting hound that I put together the other day!
Hold it right there. We still haven't decided on a theme for the masquerade ball, right?
If everyone decides to show up in whatever they feel like, that'd be way too chaotic!
That sounds perfectly fine to me. Let's not go with a theme, and let people come in whatever costume suits them.
More people can participate in whatever capacity they're comfortable with if we have fewer restrictions.
Et tu, my servant? ...I suppose I don't mind going along with that idea! Now then, my maids shall see to it that the venue is set up properly!
Looks like it's your time to shine, Old Man. "Damn straight!"
And so, the bonfire barbecue comes to a close amidst the joyous din of chatter and laughter.
In addition to the Royal Maids, a lot of the other shipgirls volunteer to help staff the masquerade ball.
(Let's see if there's anything I can do to help.)

Heading for the Polar South.say: I am resting in my room when I'm suddenly woken up by the sound of my phone ringing.
(Is Long Island slacking off again or something...)
Commander, are you awake? Can you open the door?
Royal Fortune?
The one and only! I have something important to tell you, so please open the door!
Gotcha. I'll be right there–
I throw on my clothes as quickly as possible and go to meet Royal Fortune at the door.
I've come to inform you that the cruise ship's destination has been updated, and we are now heading for the polar south!
We'll need to get through one more storm before we can leave this area though, so I'm just giving you a heads-up in advance!
I can handle a little turbulence. More importantly, how come nobody told me that we'd be leaving today?
Well, uhh, you've technically been abducted by Tempesta, right? So, of course we get to call the shots.
I guess that's true. Nobody's ever heard of the abductee getting to decide what to do and where to go.
But, surely you didn't come all the way here just to tell me that?
Is there something else you need my help with?
One sec, let me check for you... Oh, it's from Emden... A mysterious event of some sort is going to be held at the café during lunchtime. It says, "please come check it out, Commander."
And that concludes the morning news from the high seas!
After Royal Fortune leaves, I decide to head out and take a walk around the cruise ship.
When I arrive on the deck, the cruise ship is in the midst of heading to the storm's core.
A storm that manifests out of nowhere... That's worth researching. Assuming it even can be measured.
How it works in principle is beyond me, nya. I can't even get usable footage of it because of this awful interference, nya.
The ship weathers the storm without rocking too hard, then returns to the regular, tranquil seas.
While it's uncertain how much the storm affected the ship's course, what is clear is that we're in polar waters now.
As we sail further, more and more drifting ice floes become visible all around us.
Woo! Didn't expect going through a storm to be so thrilling! It was like being on a rollercoaster. Though, rollercoasters are better.
Yeah, it's completely different to speeding along with your rigging! It's fun, even if it made me a little dizzy.
It would be nice if we could have a similar attraction back at the port! Just saying!
Heroes who braved the storm, engrave this memory upon your minds. A monument shall be erected to immortalize this brave feat of ours.
I also want an attraction like that... i-if it's not too much work for the Commander, I mean...
Heheheh... Let's not forget to put the seats reeeal close together so we can rub shoulders with the Commander.
R-rub shoulders? You mean... No, no, no, no! Dirty thoughts, get out of my mind!
Oopsies. Alsace is overheating again.
Closing my eyes to that tomfoolery, I start earnestly thinking about how to build such an attraction.
I've been thinking about expanding the amusement park, and we could use another attraction or two.
I'll add it to my to-do list. Obviously, hammering out the details will have to come after we're home again.
Yippee!
While they all excitedly discuss the amusement park's expansion, the ice floats are getting more and more frequent.
At the same time, I can see the outline of an iceberg on the horizon.
(A cruise through polar waters, and an iceberg... Uh-oh.)
(No, I'm probably overthinking it. With specs like this ship has, a collision isn't even a one-in-a-million possibility.)
(Plus, we're cruising as normal and TB hasn't sounded any alarm. I'll just find something to do and forget about this.)

A Slightly Different Morning.say: The phone in my cabin won't stop ringing.
I put on my shirt, button up, then pick up the receiver.
Hiii! Ghostie here, ready to give you your schedule for today.
The ship is cruising on just as planned. Assuming nothing happens, we'll reach our destination by tomorrow.
By the way, I WAS going to give you my report in-person today, buuut...
But...?
But tonight is finally game night! I want to start practicing right away to find my flow!
Get real! You just want to play games all day!
What was that? I can't hear you! The reception is really bad here at the North Pole!
Oh, also, you should come to the arcade if you wanna participate in the game night! That's all – bye-bye!
She hangs up right on the spot.
(Ugh... Typical Long Island. Nobody told me about a "game night" until now, though. Maybe I'll check it out later.)
I exit my cabin and get on the deck. Unsurprisingly, many from the Northern Parliament are here.
(Yup. Not the least surprised to see them out here right now.)
On one side, I see Avrora, Kirov and Pamiat' surrounded by empty, rolled-over bottles. On the other, Kursk is standing alone, gazing at the ice field.
Try not to have too much or you'll be too drunk for the event later tonight.
Ah... Achoo! Someone pour me a glass!
Bleh... I brought my warmest blanket, and I'm still freezin' my ass off here! Achoo!
Have you considered going back to the cabin area to warm up?
Hell no! I can handle a little co– Achoo!
Fine. If you won't listen to me, maybe you'll listen to the Commander.
You heard the lady. Let's get you back to your cabin.
Or would you rather catch a cold and be stuck indoors for the rest of the trip?
H-h-hey! Give me room to breathe!
Okay, okay! I'll go back to my damn cabin!
Svirepy retires indoors with her blanket, after which I turn around and address all the Northern Parliament girls.
You're all out here awfully early. Have you missed the ice and the snow?
Partly, yes, but I'm also secretly hoping we'll run into new animals of some kind. Are you not hoping the same, Comrade Commander?
Yes, our motherland lies in the Arctic Circle, but this is the ANTARCTIC circle, Comrade Commander. It's a completely different experience for us.
It is amazing that life persists out here. It's a representation of nature's indomitable spirit.
Yes. Just think of the round and fluffy penguins with comparatively small feet that live here.
My heart races just imagining such strange and adorable creatures walking around!
Also, I think it's worth stating that we aren't the only ones gathered here.
All of us are here on this ship, and we've all shared such wonderful times. Most of all, it couldn't have happened without you, Comrade Commander.
I'll drink to that. Thanks for everything, Comrade Commander!
I raise this toast to you, Comrade Commander!
Where's your glass? Go get one and I'll pour you a drink!
Thanks, but I'm good. I'll just have water.
Very well. Then once again, comrades, let's give a toast to Comrade Commander! Uraaa!
Uraaa!
More of our friends gather one after another, drawn by the lively mood.
This extraordinary morning passes by in a flash as we all chat excitedly about our expectations for the destination and the aurora waiting there for us.

Wish Upon an Aurora.say: RING RING RING! RING RING RING!
There's that ringtone again. It's Memphis.
Good morning, Commander. I was tasked with briefing you today. Shall we meet at your workstation again?
Cruise Ship - Workspace
The ship has arrived at our destination. However, at this time of year, the South Pole experiences polar night. Please pay special attention to your circadian rhythm.
We plan to begin our trip to port tomorrow, so there are no particular events planned for today.
Tomorrow already, huh... It feels like we left on this vacation just yesterday.
Agreed. That just goes to show that time flies when you're having fun.
Treasure today while you still can, Commander! I'm doing the same, so I'm leaving now!
Thanks. I'll follow your lead.
With that, she leaves my workspace. After cleaning up some odds and ends, I grab my hat and step out onto the deck.
I'm instantly faced with a sky the likes of which I'd never seen before. The aurora hangs down from above as curtains of light, covering the sky and stars.
(Going with the flow is one thing, but I never expected our vacation to end this way.)
(Along the way, everyone had all sorts of fun in their own ways. That's enough to make me happy.)
Yo, Commander! You're finally here.
I was just chatting with everyone. A lot happened during this trip, but we've all had fun.
How about you? Did you enjoy it?
A lot really has happened, but I can't think of any of it that wasn't fun. I know I'm satisfied.
Ahaha, that's great! Going through the work to make this a thing was worth it, then.
Take a look over there, Commander. Everyone's got a few words for you.
Harbin pats me on the shoulder. I turn around and see everyone waving at me.
We won't bother asking if you had fun! How can one not have fun when Queen Elizabeth herself is involved?
Hehe! You just loved your vacation with Sister Sara, didn't you?
Make no mistake, Commander, your presence was a key factor in everyone having such a wonderful time.
That's right. Commander, you're our axis and our core alike. Don't forget to relax and enjoy yourself, though.
Whoa... There's something about that smile that makes me shudder.
Uh, anyway, that's not important! I'm here to thank you for helping us investigate the mysteries of the cruise, Commander!
Sooner or later, you're sure to become a legendary investigator... I gotta work harder so I can keep up with you!
Heheh. Basically, honey, everyone thinks you're the best!♪
We all got to enjoy our time together thanks to you!
Teacher... Anchorage had lots of fun! I want... to go on more and more vacations with you!
I think I speak for all of us when I say this vacation was like a dream come true, Commander. I'll cherish these memories forever.
Spending time with the Commander AND seeing all these pretty sights? Heheh, when you remember the aurora, you'd better think of me!
Seeing the beautiful aurora made the whole trip worthwhile~ But I still don't have the most important part of my collection. Commander, I'm waiting for you with open arms~♡
*yaaawn*... I don't like all the effort, but if it means getting to be with you... I can handle a little, I guess...
(Everyone really felt this way?)
Know what? I say we should end this vacation with a feast. Who's with me?
You want to start putting together a banquet now? Surely we won't make it in time.
We must let Dunkerque and the others rest, too. Would we be able to swing it without them?
Easy-peasy! We can just have a barbecue on deck.
Barbecue? Y-you want to start another bonfire...?
Don't worry! If you're that scared, then let's just barbecue over charcoal! Hell, let everyone barbecue their own food, and we won't have to ask the chefs for help.
You do have a point... Shall we go with her idea?
Yeah. Let's do it!
Alright, everyone! Let's gather on the cruise ship's garden! It's spacious enough to be perfect for a barbecue!
Everyone scampers off to prepare for the barbecue at once.
(We're going to savor this last day under the aurora, I swear it.)

The Journey's End – Or Is It?.say: Human, my darling human, it's time to wake up~ "Are you just begging for Emden's punishment?"
Morning, Emden.
In the morning, Emden comes to my bedside. By now, I've gotten wholly used to the sight of this.
Heehee♡ The cruise ship is already on its way back. But... "A new variable has appeared, human."
A variable?
(As in, a chance occurrence? To be fair, a lot of unexpected things have happened so far, but now of all times?)
Emden did promise to spirit you off to blissful paradise...
And if you so desired, it would be trivial for Emden to take the ship on just the slightest detour...
"But Emden did not cause this trouble. See for yourself, human."
I make myself presentable and step out on deck.
For some reason, Tempesta are still there. I remember they claimed that they planned to go home after we all saw the aurora together.
Well hello there, Commander~ As you can see, we won't be able to go back to our base for a while...
Surely we won't be stuck here forever, right?
I wouldn't mind... Things are less of a bother here, and I get better sleep.
Can't you just call forth another tempest and go through there? Like you did when you came here?
Commander, have you ever seen a storm near the South Pole?
Yes...?
*cough!* *cough!* Well, uh, OUR tempests can only show up when on blue seas under blue skies!
(Very well thought out excuse... Well, I'm happy to let them stay here as long as they want.)
Shame. In that case, try to withstand your homesickness and hang out with us for a few more–
WOOHOO!
After appeasing the pirates of Tempesta, I decide to make my way to the wheelhouse.
There, Emden awaits.
She's in the middle of writing something in her little notepad again.
Heehee. What did you think, my darling human? Did you find your answer?
The white Emden closes her notepad a little, tucks her hair behind her ear, and smiles...
Are the Tempesta shipgirls the only other conspirators?
Heehee, good question. "Probing for an answer now would be boorish, human."
"Just enjoy your vacation and all of its surprises♥" Emden's darling human♡
Outside the window, the aurora still dazzles in the sky.

sequence: Pleasure, Leisure, and Treasure THE END

Let the Investigation Begin.say: After a late-night call from Bristol, I come to the so-called "mystery room" at the ship's stern.
A holiday cruise, a vacation paradise.
Luxury, comfort, leisure, highlights of our time together.
But light always casts a shadow!
Bang, bang! Clap, clap! Dadadadadum!
I received a revelation within a dream. When the stars align...
Seven mysteries will come to light on this ship!
Everyone is destined to be affected! Including YOU, dear listener!
You will! Unless you make the perfect decisions at every turn, that is!
Prove to all of us that you have the sagacity and bravery to pursue the truth!
Now, join the Investigation Committee!
Come, be the detective you were born to be!
I knew you would, Commander!
I hereby declare the official formation of the Cruise Mystery-Solving Investigation Committee!
C-Commander, wait! We're just getting to the good stuff!
Ahem! I hereby declare the official formation of the Cruise Mystery-Solving Investigation Committee!
C-Commander, wait! We're just getting to the good stuff!
Ahem! I hereby declare the official formation of the Cruise Mystery-Solving Investigation Committee!
Naturally, I will be the captain and investigator numero uno! Codename: Lamplight Scholar!
Fu Shun, investigator number two, reporting in! Codename: Overlord of Adventure!
Wherever the news goes, I follow! Investigator three, Alfredo Oriani, the Turbo Journo!
Ugh, so this is why you called us all here...
Let them have their fun. Commander, let's go.
Hey, wait a sec! I really need a scribe for this one! Please!
Uuugh...
Heheh, we got ourselves a scribe for the Turbo Journo! Let's see, your codename can be...
I don't want one.
Okay, Scribe it is. Alsace, your turn!
Sacrament of the Unified Orthodoxy, an unreachable dream, guardian of the Holy See, sword of the Commander – Alsace, Cold-Blooded Killing Machine, joins the fray!
Commander, let's pursue the mysteries of this ship and corner the truth!
Heheh. I will have my fill of combat during this investigation, so Bristol gave me the nickname. One has no objections. It's a cool name!
Bristol, what did you say to convince her?
I just told her you were coming. That wasn't a lie!
So, Commander, you're investigator four! Codename: Mysterious Assistant!
Do you like it? Exciting, isn't it? Thrilling, even?!
(Honestly, I had a feeling this would happen the moment I saw Bristol and Fu Shun.)
(Mysterious Assistant, huh? It's okay for a codename, I guess. But...)
Do you actually expect crazy mysteries on a brand-new cruise ship?
Yes, yes! They'll be wild!
Help us investigate, Commander! It'll be fun!
Your involvement will make it a whole lot likelier that mysteries will pop up, too.
Come again?
A-ahem! That's just how, uh, awesome you are!
By the way, where are Fei Yuen and Albacore? You'd think they would be all over something like this...
Hush! They're in the middle of an undercover investigation!
Undercover?
They blend into crowds, listen in to conversations for any clues, and provide a compiled list of leads to us investigators.
So they're informants.
Now, I hereby declare the first Special Investigation Committee meeting a success!
Commander, keep an eye out for anything weird on the cruise starting tomorrow!
We'll let you know if we hear anything. K.I.T. – keep in touch!
Thus, the investigation of the cruise's seven mysteries began.
Forget seven, though – would there be any mysteries to investigate?

Interim Meeting.say: Bristol gave us the order to assemble, so I go back to the mystery room.
Coolly analyzing the situation, deftly navigating the scene, elegantly unveiling the truth – Detective Essex has formally joined the investigation team!
Hehe... I figured collaborating with a team would be better than working all alone.
Thank you! Together, we will seize the truth!
Hmm... Just "Detective," I guess? If that's too plain, then you can call me the Private Eye of Justice!
Now, back on topic. I'd like to reveal the clues I've collected so far.
Essex starts sticking notes she'd written on various mysteries onto the whiteboard.
Hmm... So many mysteries, in just two days.
There are even some that happened a little while ago!
Yes, and even some that I experienced firsthand. Now, as you can see, I've divided them into two groups based on time and type.
First, let's look at the mystery of the strange note.
Thanks to the tireless work of our investigators, we have three clues.
Clue the first: a note under the tray. A wet note was found under a tray in the maid café. The only legible words on it were "come" and "afar."
Clue the second: the crushed bushes. Something left a roughly 30 centimeter big imprint on a bush in the onboard garden.
Clue the third: noise in the gym. Strange sounds like a heavy object being dragged have been heard in various places around the gym.
That is all. What do you think of the incident so far, Commander?
If we want to solve this, we'll need to investigate further.
Well said, Commander. I like your firm and precise decision-making.
The note happened to end up under the tray. Everything else is coincidence and can be explained with logic.
Oh, REALLY? I think I smell a cover-up!
Could it be a giant squid monster that can write and move at supersonic speed?
...C-come again?
Whoa... That's an awesome idea! I'm voting for the Commander's hypothesis!
Ahem. Now, the second mystery from tonight – food has been disappearing.
We've collected three clues for this one, too.
Clue the first: the stolen stuffing. A container full of freshly-cut meat stuffing vanished in the blink of an eye from the kitchen.
Clue the second: the ransacked fridge. The food storage in the banquet hall was looted. Primarily food that contains meat was stolen.
Clue the third: the untouched warehouse. The warehouse, despite having more food than anywhere else on the ship, has gone completely unscathed.
Umm... Does the third one count as a clue?
We wouldn't have enough if we didn't include it, so yes!
A-ahem. Commander, a penny for your thoughts?
This still isn't enough. We need further investigation.
Again...? Surely you're being too careful.
Someone was watching too much food porn and couldn't handle the hunger pangs anymore.
Um... The meat stuffing was raw, Commander. Would any of us eat that?
Were they hankering for steak tartare? Understandable... But one must object to stealing food!
Are you serious?
What if it's a giant squid monster? But like, one that hates veggies and can hack security cameras?
A giant squid monster...?
That's an incredible conclusion, Commander. I gotta write an article about this!
Thank you for your opinions. That concludes our meeting for today!
Everyone, return to your duties and keep up the good work!

The Truth Laid Bare.say: Cruise Ship - Warehouse
Commander, Fu Shun, Essex, Alfredo, Ema, Alsace... and me, Bristol. Yep, the gang's all here.
Yeah! So? Out with it! What do we do now, Captain?
Naturally... it's time to review our third mystery!
Then why did we gather here in the warehouse instead of the mystery room?
I think we'll have a freer range of motion here. Don't worry, Commander. I'll explain everything.
We still have time before we get started, so let's review the third case: the ghost of the cabin quarter.
Once again, we have exactly three clues.
Clue the first: footsteps in the night. Mysterious footsteps can be heard from a corridor near the cabins late at night.
Clue the second: spirit photography. Alfredo Oriani got a picture of a ghost in one corridor.
Clue the third: the inescapable corridor. In one corridor, a thick fog appears and disorients people passing through.
As usual, Commander, I'd like to hear your thoughts.
Well, all of this can be explained with science. There isn't even a mystery here. It's all just dumb rumors.
Yeah! And that means that this series of events was all artificially set up!
We've already identified the suspect, too!
Water vapor, sea fog, ghosts... Tempesta has to be behind this.
Incredible, Commander... You reached that conclusion this quickly? It took us hours of discussion to figure it out.
Because the ghost might just be a giant squid monster that can disappear and spew sea fog at will.
*sigh*... Commander, now isn't the time for jokes.
I think we can all agree that Tempesta must be the culprits behind this series of events.
I've found it strange ever since the ship was attacked.
Tempesta has to pass through a storm to come to our world. Don't you find it a little convenient that they'd appear at the perfect place and time to get in our way?
This leads us naturally to one answer: Tempesta sent informants onto the cruise ship ahead of time. They coordinated the ambush.
The sounds of dragging things, strange noises, disappearing food, water vapor, and sea fog... All of it is evidence that someone with Tempesta is on board.
The clue we found while investigating the ghost is the most damning of all. Tempesta just happens to have one shipgirl who can turn invisible, does it not?
And what do you know, that same girl didn't appear during the attack today!
It's Mary Celeste!
Exactly! She's the culprit behind everything that's been happening!
Mary definitely does seem to be the likely culprit.
So, we know who the mastermind is. I still don't understand why we assembled in the warehouse today.
To catch her red-handed, of course! Heheh, Essex set a brilliant trap. Our prey should take the bait any minute now!
A trap?
Earlier, feigning small talk, I told Mary Celeste at the wharf that you would be coming to take inventory in the warehouse tonight. Alone.
She was visibly disturbed. By then, I already knew that I had her in my grasp.
Shh. Everyone but the Commander, hide. The pressure sensor I set up just went off – someone's coming.
The sound of footsteps suddenly becomes audible in the seemingly empty warehouse.
When the sound stops, suddenly, something soft presses against my back.
(Phew... I can finally be alone with the Commander...)
(Something's really wrong with this cruise ship. I can go invisible, and it's still so darn hard to get close to you!)
Ahem. Don't move! Yarrr, I be a pirate!
A silhouette emerges out of thin air. It's Mary Celeste.
Our hypothesis from before was right on the money. Mary Celeste and her pet, the giant squid(?) monster Argo, are the ones behind all of these mysteries.
*snap!*
Heheh, got my evidence!
Finally, the adventure reaches its climax!
The truth of the seven mysteries will now be unveiled!
There can only be one truth: Mary Celeste, you are the culprit!
Cold-Hearted Killing Machine, entering extermination mode!
W-wait, wait, wait! You don't have to go that far!
Hahaha... I can't believe you got me that fast. And you even set a trap... Not bad, not bad at all.
I admit defeat. You got me. What are you gonna do to me, Commander?
Hmm... Illegal boarding, food theft, attempted kidnapping...
H-huh? You're actually planning to punish me?!
I'm kidding. But it is true that your flippant actions have caused trouble for everyone else on the cruise. How do you plan to make up for that?
Heheh, easy! I've already got a plan.
A late-night bonfire party at Tempesta HQ. All are invited! How's that sound?

Mysteries Never Cease.say: According to our informants, Albacore and Fei Yuen, more mysteries have cropped up all over the cruise ship.
The aquarium, classroom, ammunition depot, and more have been affected.
Thus, the Cruise Mystery-Solving Investigation Committee holds an emergency meeting.
Ahem! Thank you all for coming on such short notice!
I trust you were all briefed on the reason for this emergency meeting on your way here!
One admits utmost surprise... How could it happen again?
Heheh. I knew we solved those last three too easily. There had to be more to it!
After all, it's supposed to be SEVEN mysteries. Three is less than seven!
Now, suspect number one, we'd like your testimony. Do you have anything to say about this?
You have the right to remain silent. Anything you say can and will be used against you in a court of law.
I didn't do anything this time! I swear on my afterlife!
This cruise has already been hijacked by Tempesta, right? We did what we came here for. Why would I have anything left to hide?
Besides, you got me on my pranks already. I'm not stupid enough to keep trying.
Hmm... You do have a point.
In that case... Is this one a real mystery?!
Or could there be another mastermind lurking in the shadows?
(I had a feeling already. This seems to confirm it.)
I'd like to confirm a few things about the chance of someone else pulling the strings.
Mary, have you heard the rumors of an inescapable corridor?
Inescapable corridor... Nope. First I've heard of it.
During our previous investigation, we discovered a certain corridor in the cabin quarters where thick sea fog caused people to lose their sense of direction. Did you set that up?
Hmm... Nah, doubt that's me.
I went through plenty of corridors when I was looking for food, and I didn't make any fog to throw people off. Never needed to.
I thought so.
Essex, if you had to choose between joining me for a drink at the open bar or continuing your work as a detective, which would you choose?
I don't even have to think! I'd choose going to the bar with you, of course!
Hmm... Okay.
(Now... We can't answer the last question here.)
(It's about the very first mystery – that note that said "come" and "afar.")
(No... I might just have my guess already.)
(But I lack evidence. I'd better observe for a little longer.)

Greetings from Afar.say: On the final night before our return, Detective Essex summons me one last time. This time, we meet on the unloading deck.
You finally arrive, Commander. Are you ready to begin our final investigation?
"Essex" smiles, waiting for my reply.
The final of the seven mysteries, of course...
...Whether Detective Essex is real or fake.
Go on, Mysterious Assistant. Tell me your hypothesis.
Confessing already? That makes things easier for me.
The real Essex did, of course, assist in the investigations. But there's no doubt that there was a fake in our midst at times.
More specifically, the Essex that came to meetings was real, while the one there for investigations was an impostor.
As for the real identity of the fake Essex...
Heheheh♪ Not bad! You didn't just arrive at the truth – you discerned everything at every step of the way.
I noticed something was off ever since we met in the gym.
When I invited you to the bar, you refused and said you wanted to keep investigating.
The real Essex would never refuse that offer.
But it was clever how you used Mary as a scapegoat.
This is a big ship, and a lot has happened. You knew that I don't have the time and energy to verify everything.
You used that fact to your advantage. There were several times when I had my doubts, but every time, my attention was diverted somewhere else.
I only realized it after we'd apprehended Mary and I was able to look back to the first night when the mysteries started cropping up.
Last night was when I first became certain that it was you – and that you'd have no reason to hide it anymore.
And so you predicted that I would summon you tonight?
Yeah. You maneuvered so I would, too, didn't you?
Heehee. I see, I see~ Why don't you call your friends for help, then?
Do you want me to do that?
Why do you think I wouldn't?
Heehee. I admit that I blundered when you invited me to the bar. I could've never imagined that was how you got me.
I'll be more careful next time.
Next time? Are you going to do this again?
Don't you want me to?
Your Observer, come from afar, bids you farewell. I had so much fun... this round.

To Celebrate the Voyage.say: When I arrive at the banquet, I find that Harbin is already there, waiting for me.
Great of you to show, Commander! Been waiting for you.
C'mere, don't just stand there. Hey, everyone! The Commander's arrived! Open 'er up!
A beautiful sight unfolds – the banquet is filled with people laughing, chatting, and generally having a good time.
There are tables lined from end to end with various cuisines. There is even an ornate dessert stand atop one of them.
Whaddya think? Really gets the stomach grumbling, doesn't it? Have a seat and chow down!
For sure! Tons of good grub livens up any party.
I had crates and crates of ingredients shipped in from the Empery to give everyone a taste of our home.
Of course, I couldn't have done it alone. All the folks who helped are the party's real MVPs! Ahahah!
The chefs 'mong us are flexing their cooking muscles in the kitchen, so all you gotta do is enjoy their food!
Hmph. I hoped to flaunt the Royal Islands' traditional cuisine, but the plebeians all insisted we keep it strictly to tea and desserts...
"They're so delicious we can't get them out of our mind," they said. Bollocks! Suit yourselves, then!
(She's complaining, but she's happy in spite of that. Thank god I won't have to eat Royal cuisine this time.)
*sigh*... And I wanted to display my cooking skills as well, but the Security Committee stopped me.
I've been practicing really hard, I promise! I just wanted you to taste how far I've come, Commander...
Even YOU don't believe me? *sob*... I'm hurt...
No, I mean that there's more than enough food as it is and I'd hate to see it go to waste. You can cook just for me another time.
Don't be sad. There's always next time.
The cruise only just started. You'll get your chance to cook soon! For now, just enjoy the party.
You mean it? You have to promise you'll eat what I make, then!
As the celebrations continue, I see Richelieu leisurely walk up to the center of the hall, wine glass in hand.
My dear friends, thank you for gathering here today to celebrate this voyage.
She will surely carry us on many luxurious pleasure cruises. Though she was built through all our efforts, none of those efforts would've been possible without the Commander's support.
I'd like to call the Commander up to give us a few words.
(Wait, what? Nobody said I'd have to deliver a speech!)
Sensing my hesitation, Bismarck in the seat next to me chimes in to encourage me.
This is your responsibility as a leader. It's okay if it's off the cuff.
I know you can handle yourself.
No backing off when the spotlight calls your name, huh?
I push my chair back a bit, pick up a wine glass, and rise to my feet.
Thank you, everyone, for creating and gathering at this place. Without you, this cruise ship would've never gotten off the ground.
I don't have anything profound to say, only this – I hope you have a relaxing, pleasant cruise.
Cheers to us all!
Cheers!
Cheers.
You too, servant. Cheers!
Cheers to us all!
Cheers!
Cheers♡
It warms my heart to see every last one of us gathered in one place.
I raise this toast for you in particular, Comrade Commander.
Don't we all? Together with you, Commander, we're all headed for a better tomorrow.
Amids clattering glasses and jubilant toasts, the party reaches its climax.
I've had my fill, so it's time to get up and have a walk around the ship.

Waterfall Free-for-All.say: I arrive at the water park at the appointed time.
There you are, honey! I've been waiting for you!
Sara will give you the rules rundown. I've got to prepare the ace up my sleeve meanwhile.
The indomitable Black Dragon has something amazing in store for you. You'll see what soon!
Without another word, New Jersey runs off in a hurry.
Hiii! Your favorite dazzling idol is here to explain the rules for you!
This time there won't be any teams – it'll be a free-for-all. That means everyone needs to try their hardest to win!
Now, normally, you'd use paintballs and score by the number of hits, buuut washing off paint is a huge pain. I was at a loss...
Until I suggested using custom-made Tribunal water guns with sensors.
Clemenceau joins the conversation, smiling and wearing sports attire.
When the gun hits a target, it will be automatically recorded on the score screen. No mess, no cleanup required.
What she said! Her idea saved me a huuuge headache.
Since it'll be a battle royale-style match, we've also set the time limit to just 30 minutes to make it interesting.
When the time is up, whoever got hit the most has to do a water-based trust fall as punishment.
Heehee. An interesting punishment. I can't wait to see how you'll do, Commander.
Wait, don't tell me... YOU'RE participating too?
Oh, no. Jean Bart is, though. She's quick on the draw, which gives her much better odds of winning than I.
Good luck, Commander. You'll need it to survive the siege that is to come.
Don't expect me to hold back on you! Heehee!
(This place is going to be a battlefield... but I'm not going down without a fight!)
My best efforts to find cover were for nothing. In less than five minutes, I'd been shot from all directions by practically everyone.
Many people pursued me recklessly, even to the point where they'd blatantly expose themselves just to get a shot at me.
(In these scenarios, I need to pick the time when I move carefully...)
Where are you looking? I'm right over here!
Uh-oh!
Move, Commander! I'll cover you!
Hey, this is a free-for-all! You're not allowed to form teams!
While Enterprise is busy drawing Saratoga's attention, New Jersey sees an opening and sneaks up behind me.
I've got you now, honey!
Pew, pew!
Even though New Jersey landed a hit on me, it could've been much worse – Enterprise's covering fire allowed me to get out of there.
On one side, Marco Polo is chasing Memphis around. On the other, Roon and Akagi are engaged in a fierce battle to the death. Rhetorically speaking.
Meanwhile, Bismarck has seized the high ground. She's picking off everyone who tries to come and get her.
Looks like I've found your hiding spot, Comrade Commander.
Ack!
Soyuz has her water gun out, but I squeeze the trigger first. My beam of water hits her hand, scoring a hit.
The fight rages on and I manage to make it to new cover, only to find that it's already occupied...
Ugh... It's just you. Don't worry, I won't shoot.
Waterfall Free-for-All.actorName: Le Malin [Mercredi at the Secret Base]

Waterfall Free-for-All.say: You're more than welcome to shoot me, though. Then I can call it quits and just sleep.
Waterfall Free-for-All.actorName: Le Malin [Mercredi at the Secret Base]

Waterfall Free-for-All.say: D-don't look at me like that! I just happened to still be here when the match started, so I guess they count me as an active participant.
Waterfall Free-for-All.actorName: Le Malin [Mercredi at the Secret Base]

Waterfall Free-for-All.say: Fighting is tiring, you know? So I thought I'd wait it out here. Can you please get it over with and shoot me already?
Waterfall Free-for-All.actorName: Le Malin [Mercredi at the Secret Base]

Waterfall Free-for-All.say: Well, if that's what you want...
I aim my water gun and casually take the shot.
Thanks. One bit of advice before I go – watch your back.
Waterfall Free-for-All.actorName: Le Malin [Mercredi at the Secret Base]

Waterfall Free-for-All.say: What's that supposed to– Aw, crap!
Got you!
Left at Jean Bart's mercy with nowhere to run, it should be no surprise that I received the most hits out of anyone.
As decided, I'm made to stand by the poolside and perform a trust fall.
I lost, so I'm just going to accept my fate!
I lean back and fall. Just as I think I'm about to land with a splash, the girls all stop my fall.
I've got you!
Heehee. I'd never betray your trust, honey!
Can you feel my warm love seeping through you, Commander?
Joyful laughter erupts beneath the clear, blue sky, and my heart beats with happiness.
I had a great time with the girls, then changed into a dry set of clothes.
There's still a long while to go before bed, so let's have another look around.

Welcome Back to Seabreeze Island.say: I've verified that there's nothing left to help with on the ship, so I've decided to make landfall on Seabreeze Island.
(This place brings back fond memories.)
(It started with a treasure hunt, and ended with all of us building vacation infrastructure here.)
(And let's not forget my exchanges with Royal Fortune. Ahh, how time flies.)
(If I remember right, this is where the turtles have made their– Huh?)
Heeelp! Laffey is drowniiing! Somebody heeelp!
She's drowning?! Hang on, I'm coming!
I run like the wind to where Javelin is pointing and find Laffey floating face-up in the water.
Help her! Quick!
(Wait... did I imagine it, or is she moving around?)
Javelin, shhh. Try to keep quiet.
(Oh, okay!)
Phew... She was just sleeping on the back of a big turtle. I was really worried there for a minute.
Let's wake her up. The turtle is heading into deep water, which actually puts her at risk of drowning.
In the midst of our exchange, Laffey opens her eyes.
Mmgh... It's you two... Did you have to wake me up?
I'm still sleepy... I'm going back to bed... Zzz...
No, Laffey! If you wanna sleep, find a safer place to do it!
I don't wanna get up... I'm not going anywhere...
(If she won't get up and do it, I'll just have to carry her there.)
Javelin and I carry Laffey away from the water and to a safer place. After that, I resume my walk.
Sometime later, I find myself near the vegetable garden.
Hey, is that...? It's the Commander.
Good day, Commander.
Hi! Do you wanna make a wreath with us? Hanazuki is really good at it!
Hanazuki, Ning Hai, and Ping Hai wave at me from the garden.
Another time, maybe! I'm on a walk right now.
I understand. Still, would you mind waiting here just a minute?
Ning, Ping, I'd like to make a big wreath as a gift for the Commander. Will you help?
- Yeah! - Let's do it!
The three of them made a beautiful wreath for me. I put it on my head then start walking again.
(I should be getting near the volcano now... Hm? Is that Marco Polo? What's she doing here?)
Volcano! It is I, Marco Polo! I have returned!
Magnificent! The monument I left to commemorate Sardegna's conquering of the island is still there!
Please don't tell me you're thinking about moving it...
That is exactly what I'm thinking! It's what you're here for! Take this monument and load it on the ship back to the port, where it may better spread my name!
Oh, you're here, Commander. You must agree that taking the monument home is a good idea, no?
She spots me and catches me off guard with a question.
Uhh...
(That thing is massively heavy. Our ship is already loaded with cargo and we'll sink dangerously low if we overburden it.)
(And if that wasn't an issue, where would you even fit it in the port?)
Marco, I have a good idea what to do with the statue.
(Where'd Clemenceau come from? I never knew she was here.)
Do you, now? Let's hear this idea of yours.
If it's prestige you want, leaving it here is the way to go.
The next time someone reaches this summit, they'll see the monument and know that you, Marco Polo, conquered this volcano long ago.
And if you want something to commemorate our visit, why not build a new statue on the ship?
It'll be light enough to transport and more meaningful than this old statue. Think about it – there was no statue on the ship before we set off, was there?
Good point... Yes, that idea has merit! Da Vinci, come with me! We must look for the perfect place to put that statue!
Come on! Chop-chop!
What? Hey! Wait!
Marco Polo and da Vinci quietly leave the volcano. Only I and Clemenceau are left.
Bravo, mastermind of the Tribunal. You found an opening and nipped the problem in the bud.
I think it only went so well because I know how what makes Marco tick.
She's actually quite easy to get along with. You know that, don't you?
I suppose so.
Anyway, enough beating around the bush. I've got a message for you.
Z23's group finished their assigned work from last time. They're doing the final checks right now.
The island's infrastructure should be almost entirely finished now.
It'll soon begin operations, producing material and serving as a supply stockpile.
Okay, and where is the paperwork? Do I need to go and find Z23?
No need. She will pass on the paperwork to Memphis, post-review. All you need to do is relax and enjoy yourself.
Clemenceau and I part ways, after which I go to a seagull roosting spot on the island.
(Ayanami isn't here, nor are there any birds. I'll just go somewhere else, then.)
An hour or two later, we all start walking back to the cruise ship.
After killing some time on the island, it's time to get back on board.

The Security Drill.say: I've come to the ammo storage, as the schedule dictates.
As the most powerful naval organization in history, our each and every battle is equally important.
The lights switch on with a click, and Sheffield steps forth from between the shadows and the light.
Even a minor mistake can prove fatal. Therefore, we must always be alert and aware of everything on the battlefield.
Next, Sheffield and Nevada also come forth.
That makes it one maid-slash-undercover-agent, one cowgirl, and one plainclothed hacker.
Of the three, only Nevada and Sheffield are carrying weapons. This motley crew really doesn't really scream, "powerful naval organization."
Ahem. I know what you're thinking. You shouldn't judge a book by its cover.
Hahah! Damn right. Just 'cause I'm dressed for the Wild West doesn't mean I can't fight!
Master, I hope you aren't harbouring doubts about our ability as soldiers.
You've more than proven your ability just by undertaking this mission.
But I'll put them on hold. You'll demonstrate your ability to me another time.
By the way, do you have any gear I can use? Relying solely on others for protection isn't ideal.
(I don't know about Memphis and Nevada, but Sheffield really likes these drills. Let's see if she'll indulge me.)
I do not. However, if you would like to have some, you are free to try to steal it from the enemy.
Good idea! Now you got me fired up.
While we've been talking, Memphis has been tapping away at the tablet in her hands.
The signal has been isolated. Area ahead, all clear.
Unit 141, commencing escort mission. Master, we will now pass through an active fire zone. Please don't stray too far from us.
Hah! You're gettin' it, young'un!
It was smooth sailing to the next safehouse after Memphis blocked the signal from Soyuz. Or so I thought...
You are surrounded! Lay down your arms and surrender!
Tsk. They've already sniffed us out...
Memphis, can't you do somethin' with them fancy-schmancy hacking skills?
They don't have any equipment hooked up to the 'net. There's nothing TO hack.
All I can do is shut down their comms channel. That'll at least slow them down.
(For Kronshtadt to do that, it can only mean one of two things...)
(One – they're bluffing and waiting for us to make a move before they launch an all-out attack.)
(Or two – they really do have us surrounded and they want to arrest all of us in one one fell swoop.)
(Whatever the case, I know one thing for certain – they still don't know exactly where we are.)
(If we can exploit that and find out where exactly THEY are, we can turn the tables on them with an ambush...)
Memphis, can you seize control of the ammo depot's security cameras?
I can, but TB will catch on to me in no time. At most we'll get 30 seconds.
That's all I need!
Using the cameras, I manage to pinpoint Kronshtadt's location. I give Sheffield and Nevada the signal to move.
Understood.
Comin' right up!
Nevada provides covering fire while Sheffield runs faster than the eye can see, getting right behind Kronshtadt.
Now! Do it!
CLACK CLACK!
I hear the distinct sound of heels hitting the ground amidst the gunfire.
Didn't you hear, Comrade Commander? You're surrounded.
(It's time for my little friend!)
I take out the stuffed Arctic hare I bought before the drill and throw it at Soyuz.
Oh!
Oh my god! It's so adorable!
THAT'S what you meant by "secret weapon"?
Well, butter my butt and call me a biscuit...
If it works, it works. Let's get out of here while we have the chance.
And so, the security drill ended with a surprising twist.
While in truth it didn't have much to do with security, everyone enjoyed it. That's what matters to me.
Time for a short break, then I'll go somewhere else on the ship.

The Masquerade Ball.say: It's time for the masquerade ball. I've come to the nightclub, wearing my usual attire.
To make the place look the part for a ball, they've adjusted the layout, replaced the ornaments, and even rolled out a red carpet.
Good evening, Master.
Scylla lifts the rims of her evening gown and gives me a gracious bow.
You chose an... interesting outfit. I can see the thought behind it.
Anyway, allow me to show you inside.
Shortly after we step inside, Hermione calls for Scylla's help, so she has to leave my side.
I decide to hide away in a corner so I can get a good look at everyone's costumes.
(Since we didn't decide on a theme, some are wearing actual costumes while others are wearing more casual party dresses.)
After a while, my phone suddenly rings. It's Kursk.
Commander, are you at the ball?
Yeah. Since you're asking about it, I'm assuming you're here, too.
I'm not. Although, I am wearing an Empery dress in the spirit of the event.
I can't stand crowded parties, simple as that. Besides, an experienced hunter prefers to create their own environment so they can approach their prey sneakily.
I'm sure you know what I mean. Bye now.
She hangs up before I can get another word in.
(Sounds like she's telling me to come find her later.)
Just as I fall deep in thought, I suddenly hear a familiar laugh.
"Ahahaha! I've finally found you, Master! I've been waiting so, sooo long for you to return..."
The Masquerade Ball.actorName: Adventure Galley [Carnival Under the Blood Moon]

The Masquerade Ball.say: Well, what do you think? My costume looks pretty smart, doesn't it?
The Masquerade Ball.actorName: Adventure Galley [Carnival Under the Blood Moon]

The Masquerade Ball.say: Adventure Galley comes and speaks to me, casually placing her giant cleaver on the ground and smiling.
There you are, my darling! I've been looking aaall over for you~
Not long after, many more Tempestas gather – Golden Hind, São Martinho, and even Royal Fortune.
Sooo... why are you wearing your regular uniform to a masquerade?
It's almost like you aren't wearing a costume at all.
I beg to differ. Wearing your regular outfit is like dressing up as yourself. That's what the Commander is doing~
So it's, like, the same, but different? You're saying the Commander IS wearing a costume?
Exactly. And we'll have to get real close to get a good look at it~
Don't mind if I do.
With all the surprise of the Grim Reaper, Mary Celeste appears and puts her prop scythe behind my neck.
She then pulls it toward her, dragging me closer.
Oh my! Mary made the first move... Well, now it's MY turn to get closer~
Oh yes, I'm going to wrap you up like a gift and make you squishy with love~
I had a dance with all the members of Tempesta, save for Whydah who stuck to a corner of the club and played video games.
(Phew... All that dancing was exhausting. I need a break.)
But before I can even find a place to sit down, something else happens.
Hoho! This is where we demonstrate our powers! Old man, let them behold! "This spectacle will leave them speechless!"
A small whirlwind whips up around Natori, lifting her feet off the ground.
The wine glasses, they're floating away! Oh no... One fell and shattered... I messed up yet again...
(Oh boy... I need to stop Natori before this gets any worse.)
I manage to bring Natori down to earth again. Edinburgh and Hermione then arrive to clean up the fragments on the floor and rebuild the wine glass tower.
While they do that, I join Épée in her little hidey-hole and sit down next to her.
It's all my fault, Commander... I can't do anything right and I only ruin things for people... *sob*...
It's not your fault at all. You did all you could to save the glasses. Only one broke, and many more would have if not for you.
Y-you mean it? It's not my fault? You really think I did all I could?
Absolutely. You're a good girl who's always trying her best.
Th-thank you... I'll keep trying my best so I can always be with you!
I comfort the sobbing Épée, putting a wrap on everything that commotion caused.
(I feel rested now. Time to get up and check out other people's costumes.)
Heehee. Not wearing a costume to a costume party is naughty, you know, Commander.
As I'm ogling the desserts at the table, Jade dressed in a witch's outfit approaches from behind and pokes me in the back.
But, if you'll dance with a good girl – i.e., me – then I might just turn a blind eye.
That's what I wanted to hear. Heehee.
Come on, who do you think you're fooling with that? You'll never be like me if you always make excuses.
Hippity hoppity! There you are, Commander! Please dance with me!
Ugh. Looks like Shimakaze brought a whole crowd of eager dancers...
Pl-please dance with me, too... Er, or don't. You probably already have way better dance partners in mind...
Roleplay mode engaged. "Urgh. Step aside, you mopey babies. I saw the Commander first."
My, my. Doesn't look like I can just stand by and await orders. Be quick, Commander – decide how this is going to go♥
I know you'll want to dance with me more than anyone else. Right, my Commander?
As I conclude my final dance, the masquerade ball, too, reaches its end.
The gentle melody gradually fades into silence, and the spotlights go out one after the next until it's all dark.
I leave the nightclub and watch as the girls retire to their cabins.
All the beautiful things that transpired tonight will linger in my mind for a long time.

The Maid Competition.say: Emden leads me to the maid café, where a scheduled event is taking place.
Welcome, Commander. This is where the maid competition is being hosted.
Following Clemenceau's introduction, I look around the café.
There is Shimanto, Felix Schultz, Boise, Marco Polo, and several others from each and every faction.
Yes. This is a competition to see who makes the best maid. Letting actual maids participate is unfair and defeats the point.
Some, like Marco Polo, are even confident that they could out-maid a real maid.
Precisely! I will show them what a Sardegnian maid can truly do.
But before that, I should give my thanks to Clemenceau for telling me about this competition.
Huh?
Heehee. Never you mind that. Allow me to explain the rules.
They're quite simple. The first maid who can make you go "ahh" is the winner.
What do you mean, "ahh"? That's awfully vague for a victory condition.
Have you heard of the Sakuran idea of "moe moe kyun magic," my dear human? "It's famous for making omelet rice taste divine."
So... the goal is really to make the best omelet rice, then?
Not entirely. It's about who wields the moe moe kyun magic with the greatest proficiency.
Thus, whoever's omelet rice makes you go "ahh" will be crowned the winner.
Emden will sit by the sidelines and observe. "Your fallible human reactions prove quite amusing."
Now then, ladies! Let the competition begin!
Clemenceau flashes a knowing smirk as she declares the competition's start, then retreats to the side.
The contestants roll up their sleeves and get to work.
O moe moe kyun magic, by your power, make this omelet rice delicious! Yeees, now just a sprinkle of this stuff...
Here you are, Commander. Savor this omelet rice topped with a little love potion. Say "ahh"!
...H-hey! What's the security doing here? Let go of meeee!
Felix Schultz has been disqualified for spiking the food!
Moe moe kyun, bless this food of mine! Huff... As a bonus, I'll warm it with my Dragon God's breath.
Oops... I blew too hard and overcooked the eggs...
That looks more like egg fried rice now... I should've known my technique wasn't sufficiently polished.
Shimanto has been disqualified for overcooking her eggs!
Huff... Puff... All that's left is to add the ketchup and recite the incantation.
Ah! I'm sorry, Commander! I didn't mean to spray the ketchup on you! Hang on, I'll wipe it off...
Boise has been disqualified for obvious reasons!
Up next was Bismarck, who simply could not bring herself to say the words, "moe moe kyun magic."
Up next was Kashino, who somehow tripped and dropped her food. And so on, and so forth, until a promising challenger stepped forth.
Hah! The time has come to show you all how it's REALLY done!
Moe moe kyun magic, imprint your deliciousness upon this dish!
Marco Polo's gestures flow like water and her exaggerated tone and phrasing hit a sweet spot. She must have practiced hard to get this good.
Heheh. So impressed, you're at a loss for words, are you? I say it's safe to declare me the winner already.
She forms a heart with her hands... while a smug grin covers her face. I doubt anyone is more pleased with her performance than herself.
Wh-what's with that look? I'm OBVIOUSLY the winner! I mean, I am, right?
(I still don't know what Clemenceau meant by "making me go 'ahh.'")
(I assume I don't literally have to say that. I'll just earnestly acknowledge how impressed I am with Marco.)
I'd say so. You win.
Really? Well, if you're sure. We have a clear winner – it's Marco Polo.
I'm surprised you're giving her the win so graciously, dear human.
Do like Marco to win. Need to write that down...
So you need only put on an exaggerated performance to distract the Commander, and in the meantime... Heehee.
Following the victor's crowning, I ate up all the omelet rice the other participants made... except for Felix Schultz's.
After that, I say goodbye to the girls and leave the maid café.
That was a lot of food... I should go on a walk to get some exercise in.

Game Night.say: The arcade is booming with video game sounds coming from home consoles, to portables, to arcade cabinets.
This can mean only one thing – it's game night.
Of course, video games aren't the only thing the arcade has. There is analog games and entertainment, too.
Table tennis, pool, card games, you name it. It's all here, and it's easy to find someone who'll play with you tonight.
Oh, you're here, Commander! If you're looking for the gamers, they're sparring with each other to warm up. Why don't you play a card game with me meanwhile?
Game Night.actorName: Fleuret [Up All Knight to Get Lucky]

Game Night.say: I won't cheat with any Sacraments. Pinky promise.
Game Night.actorName: Fleuret [Up All Knight to Get Lucky]

Game Night.say: Sure!
Fleuret wins faster than my mind can properly process.
Wooow, I didn't think you'd lose THAT fast. Wanna try again?
Game Night.actorName: Fleuret [Up All Knight to Get Lucky]

Game Night.say: Thanks, but no. I'm not cut out for these games.
Another time. I need to get in the right mindset first.
Alrighty then. I'll be right here if you wanna play again!
Game Night.actorName: Fleuret [Up All Knight to Get Lucky]

Game Night.say: I turn my back on Fleuret and walk over to the arcade cabinet section.
Commaaander! I saw that. You got completely whooped by Fleuret!
How about you enter our tournament? It's a chance to repair your tarnished pride.
Ahahaha! You barely got started before you lost! Sorry to say, but you don't stand a shadow of a chance of beating us.
Yes. I will win at any cost.
Before I decide anything, can you at least tell me what genre of game you're playing?
*yaaawn*... A fighting game, obviously? That's, like, the go-to tournament genre.
If Milord will enter, I shall pull out all the stops!
All the cabinets here are one-on-one games. How about we play each other in teams?
I approve! I propose you, I, and Pamiat' Merkuria make up one team.
So... *yaaawn*... that means me, Long Island, and Ayanami are on the other team. What prize does the winner get?
Hmm... How abooout... the winning team gets to be the first to play Shadow of the Silvertree? I know we're all hyped about it.
Heh, sounds good to me. Let's run with that.
Also, I want to put a personal wager between you and me on this match, Commander.
Let me guess – it's work-related, right?
Bingo! If I win, will you give me the next few days off?
What's in it for me if I win?
Ugh... If you win, I'll actually go to work in person and NOT slack on the job.
Sounds good. Bring it on.
Then we have a deal! Don't expect me to hold anything back!
Thus, our fierce tournament with much on the line began. Both teams nominate one player each per match.
Shortly after the first round, I managed to knock out Haguro, one of their best players.
However, Long Island and Ayanami made a swift comeback, defeating both Pamiat' and Ryuujou.
Just like that, our one-point lead became a one-point disadvantage.
(I can't let Long Island win. That's what matters.)
Round 4, let's go! I've got a hadouken with your name on it, Commander!
(Boy, she's A LOT better at fighting games than I thought... I need an opener to punish her.)
She combos my character to the edge of the screen, and she lowers her guard, thinking her victory is guaranteed.
1 HP is all you need!
Nooo! I was THIS close! THIS close!
It's okay. I will avenge you.
You've got to be kidding me...
I mashed my heart out against Long Island and my fingers cramped up. To the surprise of nobody, Ayanami beat me.
That's okay, though. While Ayanami beat me, I at least beat Long Island.
Maaan... You beat me fair and square, so I'll do some real work for once...
Waaahhh! This ghost got totally busted! Goodbye, video games – goodbye, slack time...
(I'm starting to feel bad for the poor girl... You know what? I won't be so strict.)
Fine. No days off, but you can keep "working" like you have until now.
You mean... hardly working at all? Yippee! I love you, Commander!
Let's celebrate with some more gaming!
With the conclusion of our tournament, calm returns to the once rowdy corner of the arcade.
The gamer girls aren't ready to retire yet, so they decide to stay there.
I, on the other hand, walk out of the arcade to the backdrop of Kursk teaching her pool technique to Belorussiya and Hunter.
Before I can fully exit, however, Fleuret walks up and asks me to walk her to her cabin.
(Once I've done that, I'll visit some other part of the ship.)

Barbecue Party.say: The sky above the ocean is a deep ink black.
It's strange, because looking at the clock, it's still daytime. And yet, night hangs overhead.
Amid the darkness, though, lights with striking colors dance across the sky.
There is no better place on board to watch the aurora than the garden. As soon as I set foot there, I can't help but stop and marvel at its magnificence.
(Cooking barbecue under such a beautiful sight is not on my bucket list, but now I feel like it should be.)
Pardon me, Commander! Please step aside! I need to get these fresh ingredients to Chef Lung Wu!
Oops, sorry. The aurora distracted me.
Vestal comes carrying a stainless steel soup pot, only it's not filled with soup, but an assortment of vegetables.
Let me help you with that. Also, is there any reason you've started calling her "Chef"?
Ah, that's just an inside joke. See, a bunch of us have made teams specifically for making barbecue.
Ours includes me, Roon, Lung Wu, and Hu Pen. I do the vegetables, and Roon prepares the meat.
Lung Wu is our cook, of course, and Hu Pen is her assistant. Since Lung Wu is so gifted, we naturally started calling her "Chef."
As she explains all that, she leads me to where Roon does her part.
Using a shiny knife, she slices and dices the slab of meat on her cutting board into fitting sizes.
I'd love to stay here and let loose with you, but I'm afraid that'll have to wait until after the barbecue, Commander.
Chopping meat is, to me, a kind of stress relief. Heehee.
I'll come see you a while later. Don't worry about letting me into your cabin – I already found a way to bypass the security♪
(Wait... that security breach was HER?)
Breaching attempt into your cabin's security system has been detected. Anomaly detected in ship's engine room.
(That makes so much sense. So it's her fault the ship nearly collided with an iceberg.)
Oh, would you mind taking the meat I'm done chopping to Chef Lung Wu?
Uh... Sure.
I collect Roon's diced meat, follow Vestal to where Lung Wu is, and drop it off. Then I decide to wander around a bit.
As Vestal alluded to earlier, there are quite a few teams here, all neatly preparing and grilling their ingredients.
Well, not all. Some are better cooks than others...
Ehhh... Can't we grill the eel more, you know, normally?
I think it'll be more interesting if we put a slice of cheese and some chocolate sauce on top!
I'm not sure either way... Brennus, what do you think?
If you want my opinion...
Hearing her name called, Brennus stops doing her hair and ponders the question deeply for a moment before answering.
However great the present may be, one must not neglect the pursuit of new knowledge.
The mind is particularly prone to laziness when food and drink overindulged in.
For this reason, I believe we should cover the eel with neither chocolate sauce nor cheese.
Rather, we should soak it in an energy drink!
This energy drink will not only make it delectable, it will also preserve our concentration.
Are you having a laugh? That will just make it minging!
(...I should be on my way. If they make me taste that eel, my tastebuds will never recover.)
After much ecstatic preparation, the barbecue party officially begins at last.
Here, honey! Say "ahh"!
New Jersey holds up a fork with a slice of steak. I take a bite.
Mmm. Delicious!
Heehee, of course it is! Delicious is the only kind of steak the indomitable Black Dragon can make!
Hahah! You're a damn good chef – even better than I expected! I'm no glutton, and even I can't put down my chopsticks.
Hmm... It appears to me that pouring your love into your food elevates it to a whole new level.
That does explain why the Commander is so delighted by it. It also makes me wonder what I could grill to do the same...
Meanwhile, Emanuele Pessagno and Perseus are sitting just behind us. Emanuele peers through her telescope, looking up at the aurora.
You know, to the naked eye, it looks like a balloon filled with light just burst.
Talk about a contrived analogy...
What about you, Commander? What do you see?
Ahem... Scientifically speaking, an aurora is the product of solar winds causing interference in the Earth's magnetosphere. It takes a few days for the winds to travel all that way.
Ooh! I like to think of it as the winds making that several-day-long journey just for us to enjoy this beautiful sight♪
Don't reckon the solar wind has much say in it, but that's a romantic take!
Heehee. I think we all feel mesmerized, seeing this sky right beside the Commander.
We chat the hours away until slowly but surely, the barbecue party wraps up.
(It's time to pack up. Before we start heading home, I should check if there's anything left to do.)

The Sun, the Wind, the Gym.say: I decide to pay a visit to the ship's gym.
(Huh, so it's divided into two sections. It makes sense that they'd include space for track events.)
Oh, Commander. Fancy seeing you here.
I'm just passing by. Don't mind me.
Too late for that. Hey, are you busy right now?
Not really. Why?
Heehee. Well, since you're here, let's do something fun.
Just wait here and I'll be right back.
Eugen turns on her heel and goes to the indoor part of the gym. Marseillaise is there, and Eugen whispers something to her.
Marseillaise then gets up and starts talking to everyone else inside the gym.
A short while later, a small crowd has gathered – Eugen, Marseillaise, Z46, Brünhilde, Kersaint, and Suffren.
...I am the Fighting Angel. Defeat is not an option for me.
I answer your call, Commander, and I'll bring you victory.
Sorry, what?
Did you forget what I said? We're going to do something fun.
The girls and I talked, and we settled on a three-versus-three game of beach volleyball. I wanted you to join us... but you can't have everything.
If you were playing, the team you're not on might be hesitant to go all out.
So, what am I supposed to do? Spectate?
Not quite. We'd like you to keep score for us.
Yeah. You don't have a horse in this race, so you'll tally points fair and square! That's why I'm gonna give it 100%!
For fun we play, for prize we fight. Like leopards we'll move, seize victory proud and bright.
If I j-just pray for victory, maybe, just maybe...
Hear that? They're all fired up. We're just waiting for your answer, Commander.
(There's no way this is a casual match, considering the motivation on display here...)
Let me guess... you promised something to the winners, didn't you?
Dang, the jig is up. I only promised the winning team would get to have dinner with you, that's all.
You're not going to turn them down now that we're all here, are you?
Eugen's expression screams, "I have you exactly where I want you." Admittedly, she does look brilliant in the sunlight.
With a nod, the curtains rise on a fervent game of beach volleyball.
Marseillaise leaps up into the air, hitting the ball with all her might.
With a whoosh, it flies straight toward the floor, but Z46 intercepts the ball just before it lands.
Brünhilde makes a brilliant feint, followed by Eugen jumping up high, smacking the ball with full force.
It's on course toward Kersaint, who panics and trips, thus giving a point to Iron Blood's team.
That's one point to us! Keep at it and we'll win!
The ball shall fly like a star through the sky.
I'm sorry... It's all my fault...
Don't be like that! The game has only just begun!
The Commander is watching. We must give this our all!
The motivation flowing through the players burns even brighter.
(This is getting intense. I really wonder who'll win in the end.)
Marseillaise and Suffren fought tooth and nail and finally ended the match with a draw. That means I'll have dinner with both teams.
There's still a while to go until then. I'll take the time to check out something else meanwhile.

Aquarium Over the Sea.say: I decide to have a tour of the onboard aquarium.
(It's funny when you think about it. There's a sea not just outside the ship, but inside it, too. Sort of.)
C-Commander? Did you come to look at the fish?
Well, hello! Good morning, Commander.
Oh! Would you like to go in the aquarium with us?
I was only planning to have a casual look around. Now I've run into an unexpected trio.
I accept their offer and follow them, leisurely walking through the aquarium.
Do you know why they put an aquarium of all things aboard the ship?
Hint: it's not just because Flasher suggested it!
I mean, what more is there to it?
Is it that we don't get to see fish often? I've heard not even the submarines see them much.
Umm... Is it because we all agree that fishies are cute?
Bzzzt. Wrong-o.
It's because aquariums are popular dating spots! They're nice and quiet, and the lighting is all romantic.
When you go vacationing, you need at least ONE place to go on a date.
That's why? Then it's no wonder they okayed my idea right away.
